---
title: "debiasR: OD Bias Measurement, Adjustment, and Validation"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathml
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![DEBIAS logo](../assets/debias-logo-white.JPEG){width=260px}

`debiasR` is an R package for measuring, adjusting, and validating bias in
origin-destination (OD) flow data derived from mobile phone sources. It is part
of the [DEBIAS project](https://de-bias.github.io/debias/) and links to the
wider [DEBIAS GitHub organisation](https://github.com/de-bias).

The package focuses on human mobility, where each row describes movement from an
origin area $i$ to a destination area $j$. The same workflow can also apply
to other OD systems, such as trade, internet traffic, retail flows, supply
chains, or service-use flows, provided there is a meaningful benchmark or
validation target.

## Core Aim

The central aim is to estimate bias-adjusted OD flows:

$$
F^{mpd}_{ij} \rightarrow F^{adj}_{ij}
$$

where $F^{mpd}_{ij}$ is the observed mobile-phone-derived flow from origin
$i$ to destination $j$, and $F^{adj}_{ij}$ is the adjusted flow. When a
benchmark is available, $F^{bench}_{ij}$ provides an external comparison point.

## Package Workflow

![Three-stage debiasR OD workflow](figures/debias-workflow.svg)

The tutorial sequence follows three stages.

1. Measure coverage and representativeness bias in the mobile-phone-derived OD
   source.
2. Adjust bias using a menu of methods, including coverage-based reweighting,
   benchmark-calibrated approaches, margin-constrained adjustment, and the
   Bayesian multilevel model as the main methodological innovation.
3. Validate adjusted OD flows against benchmark OD data using complementary
   fit, residual, structure, and allocation metrics.

## Notation

The vignettes use the same notation throughout:

- $i$: origin area
- $j$: destination area
- $F^{mpd}_{ij}$: observed mobile-phone-derived OD flow
- $F^{bench}_{ij}$: benchmark OD flow
- $F^{adj}_{ij}$: adjusted OD flow
- $P_i$: benchmark population or benchmark origin total
- $U_i$: observed active-user count
- $p_i = U_i / P_i$: coverage score
- $b_i = 1 - p_i$: coverage bias
- $\bar{p} = \sum_i U_i / \sum_i P_i$: global coverage score
- $e_i = p_i - \bar{p}$: coverage-score residual

## Vignette Map

1. Landing page and workflow overview
2. Why biased mobile-phone-derived mobility data matter
3. Getting set up with `debiasR` and `debiasRdata`
4. Measuring coverage bias
5. Identifying and explaining bias
6. Adjusting bias with a menu of methods
7. Validating adjusted OD flows
8. Describing the package example data
